---
format: html
title: get_anova()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>get_anova</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Get ANOVA for sjSDM Model</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Computes the ANOVA decomposition for a fitted sjSDM model,
partitioning variance explained by environmental and spatial
components.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>get_anova(mod, n_samples = 5000L, verbose = FALSE)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="mod">mod</code></td>
<td>
<p>A fitted model object of class 'sjSDM'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="n_samples">n_samples</code></td>
<td>
<p>Integer; number of Monte Carlo samples used for the variation
partitioning calculation. Larger values yield more stable
estimates but are slower. Defaults to '5000L'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="verbose">verbose</code></td>
<td>
<p>Logical; if 'TRUE', prints detailed output from the ANOVA
computation.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>The function wraps the internal 'sjSDM:::anova.sjSDM()' method.
The 'n_samples' argument controls how many Monte Carlo draws are
used to approximate the likelihoods in each variation partition.
For exploratory runs, a value of 1000 is sufficient; for
publication-quality results, 3000–5000 is recommended.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>An ANOVA result object as returned by 'sjSDM:::anova.sjSDM()',
containing variance partitioning across model components.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[fit_jsdm_model()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
